NightShift
Containment
There's never been a worse time to start a new job.
Honestly, how hard can it be to run an emergency dispatch center?
Eight Hours. Three pots of coffee. Two smoke breaks.
You'll get the hang of it. Probably.

Pre-Shift · Crown Harbor Emergency Operations
NIGHT 7 — OPERATIONAL BRIEFING
SUPERVISOR ·
The Plan
Evolution & Experience (click to expand / collapse) [ COLLAPSE ]
Unit Roster — Evolution Status
NEW CAPABILITY — MARSHAL — FORTIFY PROTOCOL Your newly evolved Police unit can run a Fortify operation on any controlled district. Fortified districts go quiet for the whole time a Marshal is fortifying it — no incidents spawn, no surprises. Use it to protect your HQ under heavy pressure, or to hold a reclaimed district while you push elsewhere. You only have one Marshal tonight. Use them deliberately and keep an eye on their fatigue while deployed.
TONIGHT'S FOCUS
⬡ ALL COMM CHANNELS IN USE
You cannot dispatch another unit until a call clears.
You currently have active channels.
⚠ HQ PERIMETER — WITHDRAWAL
"Give that order to pull back and we are all dead, there will be nothing left in their way. Nothing."
Sewer Crew — Entry Refused
Docklands / Line 7 Access Point
Field Report
Your Decision
⬡ {dispatchedUnit} — HOLDING AT ACCESS POINT
Your crew is at the access point and refusing to go further. They say something smells wrong and there's movement below the junction. Could be anything — gas pocket, animals, runoff from Foundry Ward. They want to come back up.
Docklands — Unit Dark
PW Unit — Last contact: Docklands access road
Last Known Transmissions
Your Decision
⬡ UNIT DARK — NO CONTACT
Your unit went dark at the Docklands perimeter road twenty minutes ago. No signal, no callback. The last thing they radioed was that the fence line had been breached. You have three options. None of them are good.
—
—
Incoming Report
⚠ Triage — Ask Caller for Intel
You are the dispatcher. Ask the caller for information before committing units. Each question reveals more — but the clock is running. On this shift, caller comms are reliable.

Cautious: slower arrival, lower chance of complications. Recommended for unclear scenes.
